description_long: This is a stout, polygonal wooden barrel or bucket with a wide open top filled with a blue liquid, possibly water. The body is constructed from dark brown staves, vertically aligned and reinforced by two thick, gray metallic bands—one near the rim and one near the base. The rim of the barrel is uneven, with several notched cutouts, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The metallic bands are fastened with visible bolts, adding to the industrial and robust look. The interior is smooth and appears to be watertight, suitable for holding liquids or other materials. There are no visible handles, lids, or markings, and the object is designed to rest securely on flat surfaces.
description: A rugged, dark brown wooden barrel or bucket with gray metal bands and a blue liquid inside, featuring notched cutouts along the rim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the uneven rim with a prominent notch and the blue liquid inside, with both metal bands clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a different arrangement of rim notches and the continuous metal banding, with the blue liquid still visible.
description_view_2: Another angled view showing the notched rim and the blue liquid, with the metal bands and staves in clear alignment.
description_view_3: Rear view with a less prominent notch on the rim, maintaining visibility of the blue liquid and the sturdy metal bands.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at rim and base
